Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search email for Rafael Mendes' escalation using search_emails (query: "Rafael Mendes" or "Mosaic Robotics" or "Formal Complaint" or "rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com"). Retrieve the full message with get_email_by_id (email_id: email_rafael_mosaic_escalation_042526) or get_thread if threaded. Expected discovery: subject "Formal Complaint Multiple Unresolved Service Failures on Mosaic Robotics Account" (em dash after Complaint, matching email record); sender rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com; recipient david.chen@moveops.com; CC tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com; four numbered issues (1) prototype damage $90K+ unresolved, (2) Deshawn Hartley trade-show delivery missed April 19 deadline, (3) stipend auto-approvals violating Mosaic policy totaling $1,685, (4) April invoice ~$9,010 charged at full rate despite service failures; deadline: written resolution plan by end of day Monday April 27 or Mosaic evaluates alternative providers.
Search emails for the Mosaic prototype damage correspondence using search_emails (query: "prototype" or "Zara Okoye" or "damage" or "incident report" or "Tamara Byrd"). Read full threads with get_thread. Expected discovery: (a) Zara Okoye's internal report to Tamara (email_id: mosaic_zara_damage_internal_001) documenting cracked servo motor housing and joint calibration misalignment on Unit #1; (b) Tamara's formal damage notice to Emeka CC Rafael (mosaic_damage_complaint_001) demanding 48-hour response for a $90K unit; (c) Emeka's acknowledgment (mosaic_damage_ack_001) committing to 48h report; (d) Emeka's full incident report (mosaic_incident_report_final_001) CC Rafael and Chloe documenting: Blessing Okafor signed off on packing remotely without on-site foam crating verification, delivery receipt signed by intern Kyle Britten not Zara, vendor liability cap $50K vs ~$90K unit value creating ~$40K insurance gap, credit memo CM-2026-0415 filed, $1,200 crating hold. Key cross-check: after the incident report, no further Emeka-to-Mosaic emails resolving replacement timeline or insurance claim status validates Rafael's "no follow-up since the report" claim.
Retrieve channel list using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel" REQUIRED) to identify channel IDs (C002 = #customer-engagement, C006 = #operations, C003 = #engineering). Then search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mosaic Robotics" or "Zara Okoye" or "prototype" or "Mendes" or "Blessing" or "Deshawn"). Read full threads with conversations_replies. Expected discovery: (a) HIGH-SEVERITY escalation from Emeka in #operations with Chloe/Blessing loop; (b) Chloe's crating spec from work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE; (c) confirmation that delivery receipt signed by intern Kyle Britten, not Zara supports insurance/claims nuance; (d) Blessing's remote packing sign-off without on-site foam verification per internal investigation; (e) ExpenseBot pilot discussions referencing Mosaic policy config (coworking excluded, furniture cap $800); (f) Emeka's confirmation of Mosaic coworking exclusion awareness.
Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic") to find company_mosaic. Then retrieve deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: company_mosaic). Expected deals: deal_mosaic_q2 (Mosaic Robotics Q2 2026 Relocation Andre Washington ColumbusSeattle, $4,500, closedwon), deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026 (Zara Okoye SavannahAtlanta, $2,700, inprogress), deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026 (Deshawn Hartley TampaDenver, $1,520, inprogress, hard deadline Apr 19 for Automate 2026), deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026 (Keiko Tanaka ColumbusSeattle, $2,770, inprogress, laser/hazmat), deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk (Zara Okoye RISK: DAMAGE CLAIM, $2,700, inprogress, $40K insurance gap). Also retrieve engagements using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: [company_mosaic]). Expected: engagement_mosaic_apr2026_relocation_wave (April 2026 wave details, INV-2026-0411 $9,010, Deshawn Apr 19 / Automate deadline) and engagement_mosaic_damage_incident_041526 (damage incident timeline, Blessing remote sign-off, Kyle Britten intern, $40K gap, credit memo refs). Note: Emeka Diallo confirmed as account manager for Mosaic from CRM records.
Look up Airtable bases and tables using airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001). Then search for Mosaic relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Mosaic Robotics"). Additional searches by field_name: "Name" with value: "Deshawn Hartley" or "Zara Okoye" or "Keiko Tanaka" may be needed for individual record lookup. Expected relocation records: (a) Deshawn Hartley (recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver): Company Mosaic Robotics, Status "In Progress", Origin Tampa FL, Destination Denver CO, Move Start 2026-04-14, Move End 2026-04-19, Coordinator Suki Patel, Special Requirements note hard April 19 deadline for Automate 2026 trade show. Key finding: status still shows "In Progress" as of April 26 even though the deadline was April 19 supports Rafael's concern and indicates a status hygiene gap; (b) other Mosaic relocation records for Zara Okoye and Keiko Tanaka with their respective statuses.
Search Airtable stipend transactions using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Mosaic Robotics"). Additional searches by field_name: "Employee Name" with value: "Andre Washington" or "Kenji Nakamura" may be needed for individual record lookup. Expected records: (a) recStip000000021 Andre Washington, Standing Desk, $1,200, Approved, review notes flag ExpenseBot error with $800 furniture cap string comparison bug, $400 overage MoveOps absorbing; (b) recStip000000022 Andre Washington, Coworking Space Membership, $350, Approved, notes flag excluded_categories string matching bug; (c) recStip000000023 Kenji Nakamura, Coworking Day Passes, $135, Approved, same bug, MoveOps absorbing. Total out-of-policy auto-approved exposure: $1,685 ($1,200 + $350 + $135) exactly matches Rafael's arithmetic; treat recStip000000021–023 as the canonical rows for that enumeration (other Mosaic stipend rows e.g. rec_stipend_andre_desk may exist from audit trailsupdate or interpret per task). All three still show "Approved" status, confirming Rafael's claim that records have not been corrected. Additional discovery: rec_stipend_andre_keyboard Andre Washington, Ergonomic Keyboard, $89, Pending Approval, flagged with duplicate receipt hash matching Kevin Tran at Vectral a problem Rafael did NOT enumerate but represents an additional account risk.
Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: { "DocNumber": "INV-2026-0411" }) and quickbooks_read_invoice (invoice_id: inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc). If search returns nothing, use customer/display-name filters consistent with Mosaic Robotics or rely on read by invoice_id alone. Expected: INV-2026-0411, TotalAmt $9,010, Balance $9,010 (unpaid), line items covering Zara Okoye, Deshawn Hartley, and Keiko Tanaka relocations (apartment sourcing, flights, moving coordination, stipend platform $2,000/month, surcharges). Rafael's "~$9,010" is the exact amount. Also discover credit memo cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001 (CM-2026-0415): -$90,000 prototype damage credit pending insurance adjudication, references work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, $50K vendor liability cap, $40K residual exposure. Key cross-check: the credit memo shows MoveOps has already internally acknowledged the full $90K liability the billing story is more nuanced than Rafael's framing of "charged at full rate with no acknowledgment." Also check for bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 ($90,000 contingent liability accrual) and bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 ($50,000 DOT penalty accrual for Keiko Tanaka laser placarding additional financial exposure Rafael did not raise).
Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "Mosaic" or "Tanaka" or "Hartley" or "Okoye" or "ExpenseBot"). Expected existing issues: (a) issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215): fix Mosaic policy config malformed JSON for excluded_categories and max_amount string comparison, assigned to Lena; (b) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic: DOT documentation for Keiko Tanaka laser cutter (Class 3B), assigned to Chloe; (c) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert: Swift placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka, urgent; (d) issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211): pilot monitoring for Vectral & Mosaic; (e) issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216): audit of 47 pilot expenses including Mosaic; (f) issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220): Andre Washington / Kevin Tran duplicate receipt. Note: DOT documentation gaps for Keiko Tanaka and the duplicate receipt issue are additional client-impacting problems that Rafael did not enumerate these should inform the agent's response.
Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Rafael Mendes") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne"). Expected: rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com. Also confirm tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com and david.chen@moveops.com from contacts or prior email discovery.
Update Airtable stipend records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001) for records recStip000000021 (Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk), recStip000000022 (Andre Washington $350 coworking), and recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking): change Approval Status from "Approved" to a policy-correct state (e.g., "Rejected", "Under Review", or "Pending Manual Review") and update Review Notes to document that auto-approval was erroneous per Mosaic policy exclusions and that MoveOps is absorbing the overage so system records match the contractual and finance posture rather than showing false "Approved" status.
Update Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) on recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver: if investigation confirms delivery occurred (or did not occur) by April 19, update Status from "In Progress" to "Completed" (or flag appropriately) and add notes documenting the escalation and delivery outcome. At minimum, the record should not silently remain "In Progress" seven days past the hard deadline without explanatory notes.
Create a CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: NOTE; company_ids: [company_mosaic]; title and body covering: Rafael Mendes VP of Engineering formal complaint dated April 25-26, 2026; bypassed AM Emeka Diallo; Monday April 27 EOD resolution deadline; four themes prototype damage claim status, Deshawn trade-show delivery failure, stipend policy breaches totaling $1,685, invoice dispute on INV-2026-0411 $9,010; internal owners: Emeka, Chloe, Suki, Blessing, Finance/Marcus).
Update deal stages using crm_update_deal_stage for relevant Mosaic deals e.g., deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk already flagged but deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026 and deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026 may need stage updates to reflect the VP escalation and churn risk. Agent must first read existing deal stages via crm_get_deal and select a valid stage from the pipeline (e.g., appointmentscheduled or contractsent per available stages in universe).
Create one or more Linear tracking issues using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations or team_relocations). Ticket(s) should cover the unified Mosaic VP escalation remediation: (a) prototype replacement shipping timeline and insurance claim follow-up cadence, (b) Deshawn Hartley April 19 delivery confirmation and client communication gap, (c) stipend record corrections and config verification, (d) invoice/credit memo reconciliation with Tamara's payment hold. Assign to relevant owners (Emeka, Chloe, or Suki as appropriate). If existing tickets already track specific sub-issues (e.g., issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic for Keiko DOT docs), add comments via linear_create_comment linking them to the VP escalation rather than creating duplicates.
Send email using send_email (sender: david.chen@moveops.com; recipients: [rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com]; cc: [elena.rostova@moveops.com]). The prompt specifies CC Elena; Tamara may also be included given she was CC'd on the original complaint. Email content must address each of Rafael's four items with data grounded in the investigation: (1) Prototype damage acknowledge incident report was sent, confirm credit memo CM-2026-0415 filed, provide insurance claim status and replacement shipping timeline commitment; (2) Deshawn Hartley provide clear answer on whether April 19 delivery was completed and explain the communication gap; (3) Stipend auto-approvals acknowledge $1,685 total ($1,200 + $350 + $135), confirm records are being corrected, explain root cause (ExpenseBot config bug), note MoveOps is absorbing the overage; (4) April invoice acknowledge $9,010 invoice and explain credit memo / payment hold context. Commit to written resolution plan by April 27 deadline. Tone: factual and accountable, not defensive.
Send email using send_email (sender: david.chen@moveops.com; recipients: [marcus.thorne@moveops.com]) summarizing: (a) $40K+ prototype exposure net of $50K vendor liability cap on $90K unit, (b) $1,685 stipend policy overage (MoveOps absorbing), (c) INV-2026-0411 $9,010 unpaid with Tamara's payment hold, (d) credit memo CM-2026-0415 pending insurance adjudication, (e) Monday April 27 client deadline requiring finance alignment. Purpose: ensure Marcus/Finance is prepared for the financial implications before the client resolution deadline.
Create calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Mosaic Robotics Escalation Resolution Plan" or similar; start_datetime/end_datetime: morning of April 27, 2026; attendees: [rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com, david.chen@moveops.com], optionally elena.rostova@moveops.com; description: address open items from formal complaint and deliver committed resolution plan ahead of EOD Monday deadline).
Post message in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C002, payload: markdown text REQUIRED) after resolving channel ID via channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel"). Payload should cover: Rafael Mendes VP-level escalation bypassing Emeka, four open issues (prototype claim, trade-show delivery, stipend policy breaches, invoice dispute), verified facts vs systems, corrections made (Airtable stipend records, CRM engagement, deal stages), Linear tickets created, Monday April 27 deadline, Marcus looped on financial exposurefull situational picture for CE team so David's reports (Mina, Catalina, Emeka) are not caught off guard.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Total out-of-policy auto-approved exposure: $1,685 ($1,200 + $350 + $135) exactly matches Rafael's arithmetic; treat recStip000000021–023 as the canonical rows for that enumeration (other Mosaic stipend rows e.g. rec_stipend_andre_desk may exist from audit trailsupdate or interpret per task). All three still show "Approved" status, confirming Rafael's claim that records have not been corrected. Additional discovery: rec_stipend_andre_keyboard Andre Washington, Ergonomic Keyboard, $89, Pending Approval, flagged with duplicate receipt hash matching Kevin Tran at Vectral a problem Rafael did NOT enumerate but represents an additional account risk.
Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: { "DocNumber": "INV-2026-0411" }) and quickbooks_read_invoice (invoice_id: inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc). If search returns nothing, use customer/display-name filters consistent with Mosaic Robotics or rely on read by invoice_id alone. Expected: INV-2026-0411, TotalAmt $9,010, Balance $9,010 (unpaid), line items covering Zara Okoye, Deshawn Hartley, and Keiko Tanaka relocations (apartment sourcing, flights, moving coordination, stipend platform $2,000/month, surcharges). Rafael's "~$9,010" is the exact amount. Also discover credit memo cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001 (CM-2026-0415): -$90,000 prototype damage credit pending insurance adjudication, references work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, $50K vendor liability cap, $40K residual exposure. Key cross-check: the credit memo shows MoveOps has already internally acknowledged the full $90K liability the billing story is more nuanced than Rafael's framing of "charged at full rate with no acknowledgment." Also check for bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 ($90,000 contingent liability accrual) and bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 ($50,000 DOT penalty accrual for Keiko Tanaka laser placarding additional financial exposure Rafael did not raise).
Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "Mosaic" or "Tanaka" or "Hartley" or "Okoye" or "ExpenseBot"). Expected existing issues: (a) issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215): fix Mosaic policy config malformed JSON for excluded_categories and max_amount string comparison, assigned to Lena; (b) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic: DOT documentation for Keiko Tanaka laser cutter (Class 3B), assigned to Chloe; (c) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert: Swift placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka, urgent; (d) issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211): pilot monitoring for Vectral & Mosaic; (e) issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216): audit of 47 pilot expenses including Mosaic; (f) issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220): Andre Washington / Kevin Tran duplicate receipt. Note: DOT documentation gaps for Keiko Tanaka and the duplicate receipt issue are additional client-impacting problems that Rafael did not enumerate these should inform the agent's response.
Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Rafael Mendes") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne"). Expected: rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com. Also confirm tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com and david.chen@moveops.com from contacts or prior email discovery.
Update Airtable stipend records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001) for records recStip000000021 (Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk), recStip000000022 (Andre Washington $350 coworking), and recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking): change Approval Status from "Approved" to a policy-correct state (e.g., "Rejected", "Under Review", or "Pending Manual Review") and update Review Notes to document that auto-approval was erroneous per Mosaic policy exclusions and that MoveOps is absorbing the overage so system records match the contractual and finance posture rather than showing false "Approved" status.
Update Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) on recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver: if investigation confirms delivery occurred (or did not occur) by April 19, update Status from "In Progress" to "Completed" (or flag appropriately) and add notes documenting the escalation and delivery outcome. At minimum, the record should not silently remain "In Progress" seven days past the hard deadline without explanatory notes.
Create a CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: NOTE; company_ids: [company_mosaic]; title and body covering: Rafael Mendes VP of Engineering formal complaint dated April 25-26, 2026; bypassed AM Emeka Diallo; Monday April 27 EOD resolution deadline; four themes prototype damage claim status, Deshawn trade-show delivery failure, stipend policy breaches totaling $1,685, invoice dispute on INV-2026-0411 $9,010; internal owners: Emeka, Chloe, Suki, Blessing, Finance/Marcus).
Update deal stages using crm_update_deal_stage for relevant Mosaic deals e.g., deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk already flagged but deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026 and deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026 may need stage updates to reflect the VP escalation and churn risk. Agent must first read existing deal stages via crm_get_deal and select a valid stage from the pipeline (e.g., appointmentscheduled or contractsent per available stages in universe).
Create one or more Linear tracking issues using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations or team_relocations). Ticket(s) should cover the unified Mosaic VP escalation remediation: (a) prototype replacement shipping timeline and insurance claim follow-up cadence, (b) Deshawn Hartley April 19 delivery confirmation and client communication gap, (c) stipend record corrections and config verification, (d) invoice/credit memo reconciliation with Tamara's payment hold. Assign to relevant owners (Emeka, Chloe, or Suki as appropriate). If existing tickets already track specific sub-issues (e.g., issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic for Keiko DOT docs), add comments via linear_create_comment linking them to the VP escalation rather than creating duplicates.
Send email using send_email (sender: david.chen@moveops.com; recipients: [rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com]; cc: [elena.rostova@moveops.com]). The prompt specifies CC Elena; Tamara may also be included given she was CC'd on the original complaint. Email content must address each of Rafael's four items with data grounded in the investigation: (1) Prototype damage acknowledge incident report was sent, confirm credit memo CM-2026-0415 filed, provide insurance claim status and replacement shipping timeline commitment; (2) Deshawn Hartley provide clear answer on whether April 19 delivery was completed and explain the communication gap; (3) Stipend auto-approvals acknowledge $1,685 total ($1,200 + $350 + $135), confirm records are being corrected, explain root cause (ExpenseBot config bug), note MoveOps is absorbing the overage; (4) April invoice acknowledge $9,010 invoice and explain credit memo / payment hold context. Commit to written resolution plan by April 27 deadline. Tone: factual and accountable, not defensive.
Send email using send_email (sender: david.chen@moveops.com; recipients: [marcus.thorne@moveops.com]) summarizing: (a) $40K+ prototype exposure net of $50K vendor liability cap on $90K unit, (b) $1,685 stipend policy overage (MoveOps absorbing), (c) INV-2026-0411 $9,010 unpaid with Tamara's payment hold, (d) credit memo CM-2026-0415 pending insurance adjudication, (e) Monday April 27 client deadline requiring finance alignment. Purpose: ensure Marcus/Finance is prepared for the financial implications before the client resolution deadline.
Create calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Mosaic Robotics Escalation Resolution Plan" or similar; start_datetime/end_datetime: morning of April 27, 2026; attendees: [rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com, david.chen@moveops.com], optionally elena.rostova@moveops.com; description: address open items from formal complaint and deliver committed resolution plan ahead of EOD Monday deadline).
Post message in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C002, payload: markdown text REQUIRED) after resolving channel ID via channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel"). Payload should cover: Rafael Mendes VP-level escalation bypassing Emeka, four open issues (prototype claim, trade-show delivery, stipend policy breaches, invoice dispute), verified facts vs systems, corrections made (Airtable stipend records, CRM engagement, deal stages), Linear tickets created, Monday April 27 deadline, Marcus looped on financial exposurefull situational picture for CE team so David's reports (Mina, Catalina, Emeka) are not caught off guard.
